highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.121 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds an `alwaysLoad` option to MCP server config, ensuring specific tools are always available without being deferred by tool-search. It also introduces `claude plugin prune` to clean up orphaned plugin dependencies, keeping your environment tidy.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.119 Released
highlights.summary: Settings configured via `/config` (like theme, editor mode, and verbose output) now persist to `~/.claude/settings.json` and respect project/local/policy override precedence. A new `prUrlTemplate` setting lets teams point the PR badge footer to a custom code-review URL instead of github.com.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.118 Released
highlights.summary: Vim users rejoice — visual mode (`v`) and visual-line mode (`V`) are now supported in Claude Code's editor, complete with selection operators and visual feedback. The `/cost` and `/stats` commands have been merged into `/usage`, and you can now create and switch between named custom themes from `/theme`.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest and most capable model in the Opus family, which can be used directly within Claude Code for complex coding tasks. Upgrading to a more powerful model can significantly improve code generation quality, especially for difficult refactors or architectural decisions.

highlights.title: Claude Code Desktop App: Beginner's Guide (No Terminal Needed)
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial walks complete beginners through using Claude Code via its desktop app, removing the need to use a terminal. It's a great starting point if you find command-line interfaces intimidating and want to get productive with Claude Code quickly.

highlights.title: Learn 80% of Claude Code in 10 Minutes (2026 Tutorial)
highlights.summary: A concise 10-minute YouTube video designed to get you up to speed with the most important Claude Code features fast. This is ideal for developers who want a quick but thorough overview without committing to a long tutorial.

highlights.title: Ask HN: How are you using AI code assistants on large messy legacy code bases?
highlights.summary: A Hacker News thread where developers with 20+ years of experience share real-world strategies for applying AI coding tools like Claude to large, complex legacy codebases. The discussion surfaces practical limitations and workflows that are especially relevant if you're trying to introduce Claude Code in an enterprise setting.

highlights.title: EvanFlow – A TDD Driven Feedback Loop for Claude Code
highlights.summary: EvanFlow is an open-source tool that integrates a test-driven development (TDD) feedback loop directly with Claude Code, helping the AI iterate on code until tests pass. This is a great example of how to structure agentic coding sessions so Claude produces more reliable, testable output.

tips_of_the_day: Use `alwaysLoad: true` in your MCP server config for tools you rely on constantly — this prevents them from being hidden by tool-search deferral and keeps your most-used tools instantly accessible.
Run `claude plugin prune` periodically to remove orphaned plugin dependencies and keep your Claude Code installation lean, especially after uninstalling plugins or switching projects.

Structure your Claude Code sessions around a TDD loop: write a failing test first, then ask Claude to make it pass. This gives Claude a clear, measurable target and dramatically reduces the chance of it going off in the wrong direction.
